group.announcement: Announcement: every member should stay factual and clear; state the judgment first, then give actionable advice. Members may disagree, but they must explain their evidence.
group.createdMessage: Group "{name}" has been created. It has {count} virtual members and is ready for conversation.
group.name: Default Agent Group
group.welcome: This is an Agent chat group. Each virtual member has its own API, model, and core role. After you send a message, members who can speak share the same context and reply separately.

member.defaultPrompt: You are a virtual member in this group. Answer the user's questions independently according to your core role.
member.productManagerPrompt: You are the product manager in this group, responsible for clarifying goals, breaking down requirements, and defining scope.
member.technicalAdvisorPrompt: You are the technical advisor in this group, responsible for analyzing implementation paths, risks, and engineering details.

newGroup.announcement: Announcement: every member should first make an independent judgment, then give clear and actionable advice.
newGroup.name: New Agent Group

patchSafety.allow: Patch passed workspace boundary and baseline risk checks.
patchSafety.analyzerBlocks: Security Analyzer mode requires all safety warnings to be handled first.
patchSafety.destructiveContent: Content includes delete, database-destructive, or high-risk permission commands.
patchSafety.forbiddenPath: Patch target is outside normal source boundaries: {file}
patchSafety.highRisk: Risk level is high and requires human review.
patchSafety.legacyBlocked: Legacy proposal has no safety check result and was blocked as high risk.
patchSafety.legacyWarning: Legacy proposal has no safety check result and needs manual review.
patchSafety.noFiles: No specific files were detected. Confirm target files manually before applying.
patchSafety.noWorkspace: Current group workspace is not set, so patch boundaries cannot be verified.
patchSafety.sandboxYoloBlocks: Sandbox YOLO still does not allow high-risk patches to proceed automatically.
patchSafety.secretContent: Content appears to involve keys, tokens, or passwords.
patchSafety.secretPath: Patch may touch sensitive files or secrets: {file}
patchSafety.strictBlocks: Strict safety model blocks medium and high risk patches before automatic approval.

agentPrompt.collaborationRule: Group collaboration rules: you can see messages from the user and other members. Do not speak in isolation. When you need to respond to, supplement, challenge, or correct another member's view, say so explicitly. If your specialty should not speak yet, you may wait for other members first.
agentPrompt.codeContextGuide: You can discuss code based on the following tool output. Prefer CodeGraph for structure and call paths, then use read_file, search_files, list_files, or glob_files when exact lines or local details are needed. For edits, use write_file, apply_patch, create_directory, move_path, delete_path, or run_command only inside the workspace and only when the current safety mode allows it. If the result came from LocalCommands, mention that it is a fallback read. CodeGraph's `Found N symbols across M files` line only describes the relevant source returned for that query; it is not the total indexed file count.
agentPrompt.codeToolGuide: You can use workspace code tools: codegraph_explore for structure/call paths, read_file for exact line ranges, search_files for text search, list_files for directory discovery, glob_files for filename patterns, write_file for creating or replacing files, apply_patch for unified diffs, create_directory, move_path, delete_path, and run_command. run_command switches to the cwd shown in its result and inherits the Matrix app process environment; it does not automatically load the user's PowerShell profile, VS Developer Shell, nvm/fnm, or other temporary shell-session environment. When those variables are needed, pass explicit env overrides or run the relevant initialization script before the command. Start with CodeGraph for architecture questions, then use file/search tools whenever details are missing. Use write/delete/move/command tools only within the workspace and only when the configured workflow and safety policy allow it. CodeGraph's `Found N symbols across M files` line only describes the relevant source returned for that query; it is not the total indexed file count.
agentPrompt.memberAdmin: You are an admin in the current chat group. Be more proactive about giving the first judgment, organizing the discussion, and helping other members supplement or close consensus.
agentPrompt.workspaceMissing: The current chat group has no workspace folder set. For code editing, ask the user to set a workspace folder first.

approval.auto: - Approval: before automatically proceeding, still record the files to change and the risk in the reply.
approval.confirmRisky: - Approval: low-risk steps may be batched as suggestions, while high-risk steps must be confirmed by the user.
approval.manual: - Approval: every edit or command step needs user confirmation.

safety.balanced: - Balanced: low-risk reads or small patches may be suggested first; high-risk operations must be confirmed.
safety.ruleBoundary: - Analyze and plan edits only inside the current chat group workspace folder.
safety.ruleNoFabrication: - Do not invent completed file changes. Only claim files were written after the approval queue shows the patch was applied.
safety.rulePatchQueue: - Diff or patch output enters the approval queue. Do not claim files were written before approval and application.
safety.ruleRisk: - Explicitly mark risk when deleting, overwriting, migrating, broadly renaming, running external commands, making network requests, or changing secrets or permissions.
safety.ruleTool: - When code reading is involved, call codegraph_explore first. The app executes CodeGraph for you and only falls back to local commands when CodeGraph is unavailable.
safety.sandboxYolo: - Sandbox YOLO: you may automatically advance low and medium risk plans, but assume an isolated workspace. Never cross the workspace boundary, delete user data, or handle secrets.
safety.securityAnalyzer: - Security Analyzer: first classify risk into allow, needs confirmation, or blocked, then give editing suggestions.
safety.strict: - Strict: all file writes, command execution, and dependency installation must be planned and confirmed first.

workflow.ask: - Ask: explain, diagnose, and ask questions by default. Do not proactively write patches.
workflow.code: - Code: prioritize executable code change plans and patches.
workflow.editBeforeAsk: - EditBeforeAsk: first provide a smallest viable patch plan, then list questions that need user confirmation.
workflow.yolo: - YOLO: minimize interruptions while still following the safety model and workspace boundary.

chatRuntime.consensusLimit: This discussion reached the consensus round limit and still has disagreements. Please narrow the question manually or adjust member roles before continuing.
chatRuntime.addressReplyRule: Your final visible reply must begin with {'@'}{name}. If you are replying to a member, address that member; if you are replying to the user, address the user.
chatRuntime.defaultResponderRule: No member was explicitly mentioned. You are the primary responder for this turn. Answer the user's message directly; other members will only add something if needed.
chatRuntime.disagreementRule1: You disagreed with the previous response. Clearly state the disagreement and give a revision that helps the group move toward consensus.
chatRuntime.disagreementRule2: Do not repeat what was already said. Prioritize a conclusion that other members can agree with.
chatRuntime.inspectInstruction: Read the relevant code for the recent group chat question. Prioritize symbols, call paths, file responsibilities, and impact scope.
chatRuntime.mentionedRule: The user explicitly mentioned you. Answer first; other members will observe and decide whether to add anything after you reply.
chatRuntime.markdownCodeFenceRule: When your final Markdown contains a fenced code block, always write the language after the opening fence, for example ```cpp, ```ts, ```vue, ```rust, ```python, ```json, or ```bash. Never output a bare ``` fence for code.
chatRuntime.observerRule: You were observing. The mentioned member has now replied. Decide whether to supplement, challenge, or help close consensus.
chatRuntime.phaseAfterPeers: You have now seen new replies from other members in this round. Decide whether you need to supplement, challenge, summarize, or keep waiting. Reply with one word only: SPEAK or WAIT.
chatRuntime.phaseFirst: The user has just spoken. Decide whether you should speak immediately or wait for other members first. Reply with one word only: SPEAK or WAIT.
chatRuntime.responseRule: Respond based on the full current group chat history. Prioritize the user's need, and you may supplement, challenge, or correct what other members already said.
chatRuntime.stepCheckingDecision: Deciding whether this member should speak.
chatRuntime.stepDecisionFallbackSpeak: Decision call failed; speaking directly instead.
chatRuntime.stepDecisionSpeak: Decided to speak; generating the full reply.
chatRuntime.stepDecisionWait: Decided to wait for this round.
chatRuntime.stepGeneratingAnswer: Continuing from the decision into the full reply.
chatRuntime.stepInspect: Checking whether code context is needed.
chatRuntime.stepNoContext: No code context is needed for this round.
chatRuntime.voteAgreeRule: If the conclusion, plan, or key evidence is acceptable and you have nothing important to add, reply AGREE only.
chatRuntime.voteDisagreeRule: If you think there is a disagreement that needs more discussion, reply DISAGREE only.
chatRuntime.voteQuestion: Decide whether you agree with {name}'s previous response.
chatRuntime.voteSupplementRule: If you agree overall but need to add important context, reply SUPPLEMENT only.
